title: Leapfrog preserves a scalar discrete invariant

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For the scalar leapfrog recurrence x_{n+2} = 2 x_{n+1} - x_n - dt^2 omega^2 x_{n+1}, with dt nonzero, the quantity ((x_{n+1} - x_n) / dt)^2 + omega^2 x_{n+1} x_n is independent of n.
informal.latex: Let \(\omega,\Delta t\in\mathbb{R}\) with \(\Delta t\ne 0\). If a sequence \((x_n)_{n\ge 0}\) satisfies \(x_{n+2}=2x_{n+1}-x_n-(\Delta t)^2\omega^2x_{n+1}\), then \(I_n=((x_{n+1}-x_n)/\Delta t)^2+\omega^2x_{n+1}x_n\) satisfies \(I_n=I_0\) for all \(n\in\mathbb{N}\).
assumptions: omega and dt are real numbers
x is a real-valued sequence indexed by natural numbers
x satisfies the scalar leapfrog recurrence for every natural-number index
informal.expected_conclusion: The leapfrog discrete invariant is constant along the indexed orbit.

declarations.name: leapfrogStep
declarations.statement: def leapfrogStep (omega dt x xPrev : ℝ) : ℝ := 2 * x - xPrev - dt ^ 2 * omega ^ 2 * x

declarations.name: leapfrogInvariant
declarations.statement: def leapfrogInvariant (omega dt x xPrev : ℝ) : ℝ := ((x - xPrev) / dt) ^ 2 + omega ^ 2 * x * xPrev

declarations.name: IsLeapfrogOrbit
declarations.statement: def IsLeapfrogOrbit (omega dt : ℝ) (x : ℕ → ℝ) : Prop := ∀ n : ℕ, x (n + 2) = leapfrogStep omega dt (x (n + 1)) (x n)

declarations.name: leapfrogInvariant_const_of_isLeapfrogOrbit
declarations.statement: theorem leapfrogInvariant_const_of_isLeapfrogOrbit (omega dt : ℝ) (hdt : dt ≠ 0) (x : ℕ → ℝ) (hx : IsLeapfrogOrbit omega dt x) : ∀ n : ℕ, leapfrogInvariant omega dt (x (n + 1)) (x n) = leapfrogInvariant omega dt (x 1) (x 0)

tasks.input: Translate the informal LaTeX statement into a Lean 4 theorem statement using Mathlib.
tasks.target: theorem leapfrogInvariant_const_of_isLeapfrogOrbit (omega dt : ℝ) (hdt : dt ≠ 0) (x : ℕ → ℝ) (hx : IsLeapfrogOrbit omega dt x) : ∀ n : ℕ, leapfrogInvariant omega dt (x (n + 1)) (x n) = leapfrogInvariant omega dt (x 1) (x 0)

tasks.input: Given the formal declarations and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using the one-step invariant theorem and induction on n.
